name: Willis Conover
abstract: Willis Clark Conover, Jr. (December 18, 1920 – May 17, 1996) was an American jazz producer and broadcaster on the Voice of America for more than forty years.

concerts.label: Sun Ra and His Space Arkestra

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The late Willis Conover was a one- man movement to get jazz included in what was then the rather new arts endowment... Willis went patiently behind the scenes and managed to put together a small of people, including Dizzy Gillespie and the late John Gensel... Orrin Keepnews and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Josef Balcerak
about_quotes.evidence: And I must tell you that the great success for Polish jazz generation was the first visit of Willis Conover... Willis Conover came as a first time. He was very surprised by the grouse, versatility, and liveliness of the Polish jazz scene.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: And it came on every night out of Washington with Willis Conover. And he played mostly jazz. And the average Russian, when he speaks English, sounds like Willis Conover.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, Connecticut, November 21, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: They asked me, along with Willis Conover – and he simply said he didn’t want to do it – so I was the first chairman of the advisory committee on jazz to Kennedy Center.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: There were a lot of people who were jealous and envious of me, wanted my job: Willis Conover, Gorge Avakian. They were't friends of mine. They were not friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Galloway
about_quotes.evidence: I also was able to tune in the Voice of America with Willis Conover and that’s where I heard a huge amount of the great jazz that I became so familiar with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Galloway interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998
